rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical radical cyclization of N-aryl acrylamides with polyhalomethanes (e.g., BrCHCl2, CH2I2) to give 3,3-disubstituted oxindoles bearing haloalkyl groups
electrochemical_role: Cathodic reduction of the polyhalomethane (e.g., Br-CHCl2) generates a haloalkyl radical (·CHCl2) via C-Br bond cleavage; this radical adds to the acrylamide alkene, and the resulting C-centered radical cyclizes onto the arene to form the oxindole after rearomatization

key_intermediates.name: tertiary C-radical from radical addition to acrylamide alkene
cyclohexadienyl radical after ipso/ortho cyclization onto arene

electron_flow: Cathode reduces the C-X bond of the polyhalomethane to release a haloalkyl radical; this radical adds across the acrylamide C=C, the resulting α-amido carbon radical undergoes intramolecular homolytic aromatic substitution onto the N-aryl ring, and anodic oxidation/deprotonation rearomatizes the cyclohexadienyl radical to deliver the oxindole.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction yield drops from 78% to <2% in the presence of 5 equiv TEMPO, consistent with a radical pathway

notes: Radical trapping with TEMPO essentially shuts down the reaction, supporting radical intermediates. Cyclization products bearing CH2I (compound 39) and CHCl2 (compound 3) were isolated, and a deuterated analogue (compound 48) was prepared en route to deuterated Lansai B, providing isotope-labeling support for the proposed radical addition/cyclization sequence.
